%% N: number of rows
%% M: number of columns
%% Sources: list of [r_i, c_i, color_i]
solve(N, M, Sources) ->
    % State for each cell (r, c) will be {Color, Time}
    % Initialize with {0, infinity} (0 color means uncolored)
    infinity = 1000000000, % A sufficiently large number for infinity time
    
    Grid = lists:nth(0, N, lists:map(fun(_ -> {0, infinity}); _end)),
    
    % Initialize queue: list of {R, C, Color, Time}
    InitialQueue = [],
    
    % Initialize grid and queue with sources
    {SourcesGrid, InitialQueue} = lists:foldl(
        fun({CurrentGrid, CurrentQueue}, [R, C, Color]) ->
            % Update Grid: {Color, Time}
            NewGrid = lists:nth(R, CurrentGrid, 
                lists:map(fun(Cell) -> 
                    if Cell = {0, infinity} -> {Color, 0}; true -> Cell end
                end, lists:nth(C, lists:nth(R, CurrentGrid)))
            ),
            
            % Update Queue
            NewQueue = [{R, C, Color, 0} | CurrentQueue],
            
            {NewGrid, NewQueue}
        end,
        {Grid, InitialQueue},
        Sources
    ),
    
    % Use BFS approach: Queue stores elements to process.
    % We use a functional queue structure (list append/head)
    BFS_State = {SourcesGrid, InitialQueue},
    
    % Perform BFS
    {FinalGrid, _} = bfs_loop(N, M, BFS_State),
    
    % Format output: extract only the color component
    ResultGrid = lists:map(fun(Row) -> 
        lists:map(fun({Color, _Time}) -> Color end, Row)
    end, FinalGrid),
    
    ResultGrid.

-module(solver_module).

solve(N, M, Sources) ->
    % Initialize the state grid: [[Color, Time_of_Arrival]]
    % Use {0, infinity} for uncolored/unreached. Colors are >= 1.
    infinity = 1000000000, % A sufficiently large number for infinity
    
    Grid = lists:replicate(N, lists:replicate(M, {0, infinity})),

    % Initialize the queue for BFS
    Queue = [],
    
    % Process initial sources
    {InitialGrid, InitialQueue} = lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, [R, C, Color]) ->
            case Acc#Grid of
                {Grid, Queue} ->
                    % Update grid at (R, C) to {Color, 0}
                    NewGrid = lists:update_2d(Grid, R, C, {Color, 0}),
                    % Add source to queue: {R, C, Color, Time}
                    NewQueue = [{R, C, Color, 0} | Queue]
                    {NewGrid, NewQueue}
            end
        end,
        {Grid, Queue},
        Sources
    ),

    % Run BFS
    FinalGrid = bfs(N, M, InitialGrid, InitialQueue),

    % Extract colors from the final state
    Result = lists:map(fun(Row) -> 
        lists:map(fun({Color, _Time}) -> Color end, Row)
    end, FinalGrid),
    
    Result.

%% Helper function to update a value in a 2D list structure
update_2d(Grid, R, C, Value) ->
    lists:insert_at(R, lists:update_at(Grid !! R, C, Value))
    where
        update_at(List, Index, Value) ->
            lists:insert_at(Index, Value, List)

%% BFS core logic
bfs(N, M, Grid, Queue) ->
    % We use a queue structure that allows efficient dequeue from the front.
    % Since Erlang lists are efficient for prepending/appending, we'll treat 
    % the list as a queue where we append to the end and process from the front.
    
    bfs_loop(N, M, Grid, Queue).

bfs_loop(N, M, Grid, [] ) ->
    Grid;

bfs_loop(N, M, Grid, [Current | Rest]) ->
    case Current of
        {R, C, Color, Time} ->
            % Explore neighbors
            Neighbors = [
                {R, C + 1}, {R, C - 1}, {R + 1, C}, {R - 1, C}
            ],
            
            {NewGrid, NewQueue} = lists:foldl(
                fun(Acc, {NR, NC}) ->
                    case Acc#Grid of
                        {Grid, Queue} ->
                            NextTime = Time + 1,
                            
                            if NR >= 0 and NR < N and NC >= 0 and NC < M ->
                                Case = Grid !! NR !! NC,
                                {ExistingColor, ExistingTime} = Case,

                                if ExistingTime == infinity ->

                                % Case 2: Reached at the same time
                                else if NextTime == ExistingTime ->
                                    if Color > ExistingColor ->
                                        NewGrid' = lists:update_2d(Grid, NR, NC, {Color, NextTime}),
                                        % No need to re-queue if we update at the same time step
                                        {Grid, Queue}
                                    else
                                        {Grid, Queue}
                                    end
                                % Case 3: Faster path found
                                else if NextTime < ExistingTime ->
                                    NewGrid' = lists:update_2d(Grid, NR, NC, {Color, NextTime}),
                                    NewQueue' = [{NR, NC, Color, NextTime} | Queue]
                                    {NewGrid', NewQueue'}
                                % Case 4: Slower path, ignore
                                else
                                    {Grid, Queue}
                            else
                                {Grid, Queue} % Out of bounds
                            end
                end,
                {Grid, Rest},
                Neighbors
            ),
            
            % Continue BFS with the rest of the queue plus newly discovered nodes
            bfs_loop(N, M, NewGrid, NewQueue ++ Rest).
